mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, labor market slack severely constrains consumer spending growth and corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and builder confidence, creating a compounding demand shock that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or positive return frequency falls below 45% when both series trend upward concurrently over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate expanding factory utilization and immediate revenue generation, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week specifically during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during expansions, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with p-values exceeding 0.10 when INDPRO trends upward.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms robust aggregate demand, prompting immediate equity market repricing and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency drops below 50% when GDP growth surpasses the threshold, or the signal exhibits negative autocorrelation in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production stagnates while the consumer price index exceeds 220, stagflationary pressures erode real corporate profits and trigger central bank tightening fears, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns, or positive return frequency exceeds 60% during these periods across any 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly nonfarm payroll additions above 150,000 signal strengthening labor demand and rising household income, which drives consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.02 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of near-zero policy rates and accelerating monthly inflation creates expectations of future monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns, or positive return frequency exceeds 55% when both conditions are met during out-of-sample validation.
